content_en:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah is organized into eight sections (aqsam) that reflect Ibn Hajar's precise methodological classification of individuals associated with the Prophet Muhammad. The first and most important section covers those who are established as Companions by reliable evidence. The second covers those who are mentioned in the texts only as Companions without further documentation. The third covers those about whom there is doubt whether they are Companions or Successors. The fourth covers those who have been misidentified as Companions. Additional sections cover women Companions, children who met the Prophet, and those who converted after the Prophet's death but during the period of the Companions.

This careful classificatory structure reflects Ibn Hajar's hadith-critical methodology. The definition of a Sahabi (Companion) is not merely anyone who met the Prophet — it specifically means someone who met the Prophet while believing in him and died as a Muslim. Ibn Hajar's classification scheme allows him to include everyone associated with the Prophet while clearly marking the degree of certainty about each person's status.

For each entry, Ibn Hajar follows a standard format: the person's name and genealogy, how they are connected to the Prophet, the relevant hadith and historical reports documenting this connection, scholarly assessments of those reports' reliability, and any biographical information available about the person's life and activities. Entries range from a few lines for obscure individuals to many pages for major Companions.

Ibn Hajar's critical apparatus — his evaluation of the reliability of sources, his identification of errors in earlier works, his reconciliation of conflicting accounts — is applied throughout. He regularly corrects mistakes in earlier treatments of the Companions, notes when accounts are confused or when two individuals have been merged into one, and evaluates the evidence with the technical precision of the hadith sciences.

The alphabetical arrangement within each section facilitates reference use. Ibn Hajar includes separate alphabetical sections for individuals known by their given names, those known by their fathers' names, those known by their mothers' names, and those known by a nickname (kunya or laqab). This multi-index approach ensures that a person can be found regardless of how they are typically referenced.

The work draws on a vast bibliography. Ibn Hajar cites hundreds of earlier sources — the major hadith collections, specialized biographical works on the Companions, local histories, and general biographical dictionaries — synthesizing information scattered across an enormous literature into a single comprehensive reference.
content_id:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah disusun dalam delapan bagian (aqsam) yang mencerminkan klasifikasi metodologis yang tepat dari Ibnu Hajar tentang individu-individu yang terkait dengan Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Bagian pertama dan terpenting mencakup mereka yang ditetapkan sebagai Sahabat berdasarkan bukti yang dapat dipercaya. Bagian kedua mencakup mereka yang disebutkan dalam teks-teks hanya sebagai Sahabat tanpa dokumentasi lebih lanjut. Bagian ketiga mencakup mereka yang masih diragukan apakah mereka Sahabat atau Tabi'in. Bagian keempat mencakup mereka yang telah salah diidentifikasi sebagai Sahabat. Bagian tambahan mencakup Sahabat wanita, anak-anak yang bertemu Nabi, dan mereka yang masuk Islam setelah kematian Nabi tetapi selama masa Sahabat.

Struktur klasifikasi yang cermat ini mencerminkan metodologi kritik hadis Ibnu Hajar. Definisi Sahabi (Sahabat) bukan sekadar siapa pun yang bertemu Nabi ﷺ — secara khusus berarti seseorang yang bertemu Nabi ﷺ saat beriman kepada beliau dan meninggal sebagai Muslim. Skema klasifikasi Ibnu Hajar memungkinkannya memasukkan semua orang yang terkait dengan Nabi ﷺ sambil dengan jelas menandai tingkat kepastian tentang status masing-masing.

Untuk setiap entri, Ibnu Hajar mengikuti format standar: nama dan nasab orang tersebut, bagaimana mereka terhubung dengan Nabi ﷺ, hadis dan laporan historis yang relevan yang mendokumentasikan hubungan ini, penilaian ulama tentang keandalan laporan tersebut, dan informasi biografis apa pun yang tersedia tentang kehidupan dan aktivitas orang tersebut.

Perangkat kritis Ibnu Hajar — evaluasinya tentang keandalan sumber, identifikasinya tentang kesalahan dalam karya-karya sebelumnya, rekonsiliasi akun-akun yang bertentangan — diterapkan di seluruh karya. Ia secara teratur mengoreksi kesalahan dalam perlakuan sebelumnya tentang para Sahabat, mencatat ketika akun-akun membingungkan atau ketika dua individu telah digabungkan menjadi satu, dan mengevaluasi bukti dengan presisi teknis ilmu hadis.

Susunan alfabetis dalam setiap bagian memfasilitasi penggunaan referensi. Karya ini juga mencakup bagian-bagian alfabetis terpisah untuk individu yang dikenal dengan nama yang diberikan, mereka yang dikenal dengan nama ayah, nama ibu, dan julukan (kunya atau laqab). Pendekatan multi-indeks ini memastikan bahwa seseorang dapat ditemukan terlepas dari cara mereka biasanya dirujuk.
content_ar:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah is organized into eight sections (aqsam) that reflect Ibn Hajar's precise methodological classification of individuals associated with النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم Muhammad. The first and most important section covers those who are established as Companions by reliable evidence. The second covers those who are mentioned in the texts only as Companions without further documentation. The third covers those about whom there is doubt whether they are Companions or Successors. The fourth covers those who have been misidentified as Companions. Additional sections cover women Companions, children who met النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم, and those who converted after النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم's death but during the period of the Companions.

This careful classificatory structure reflects Ibn Hajar's hadith-critical methodology. The definition of a Sahabi (Companion) is not merely anyone who met النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم — it specifically means someone who met النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم while believing in him and died as a Muslim. Ibn Hajar's classification scheme allows him to include everyone associated with النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم while clearly marking the degree of certainty about each person's status.

For each entry, Ibn Hajar follows a standard format: the person's name and genealogy, how they are connected to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم, the relevant hadith and historical reports documenting this connection, scholarly assessments of those reports' reliability, and any biographical information available about the person's life and activities. Entries range from a few lines for obscure individuals to many pages for major Companions.

Ibn Hajar's critical apparatus — his evaluation of the reliability of sources, his identification of errors in earlier works, his reconciliation of conflicting accounts — is applied throughout. He regularly corrects mistakes in earlier treatments of the Companions, notes when accounts are confused or when two individuals have been merged into one, and evaluates the evidence with the technical precision of the hadith sciences.

The alphabetical arrangement within each section facilitates reference use. Ibn Hajar includes separate alphabetical sections for individuals known by their given names, those known by their fathers' names, those known by their mothers' names, and those known by a nickname (kunya or laqab). This multi-index approach ensures that a person can be found regardless of how they are typically referenced.

The work draws on a vast bibliography. Ibn Hajar cites hundreds of earlier sources — the major hadith collections, specialized biographical works on the Companions, local histories, and general biographical dictionaries — synthesizing information scattered across an enormous literature into a single comprehensive reference.
